heroHeadline:
Israel Strikes South Pars Gas Field — Historic First Upstream Strike; Intel Minister Khatib Killed;
B-1 Bombers Enter Iranian Airspace; Oil Surges Back to $109
heroSubtitle: Day 20: Israel struck South Pars gas field (world's largest natural gas reserve) — the first direct attack on Iran's upstream gas infrastructure, pushing Brent crude back to ~$108–110/bbl. Israel killed Intelligence Minister Esmail Khatib. US B-1 Lancer bombers were observed flying directly over Iranian airspace, signaling near-total air dominance. USS Tripoli ARG with 31st MEU (~2,200 Marines) heading to Middle East. Iran attacked all six GCC states simultaneously for the first time in history. IMO emergency council meeting convened — UN maritime body seeking humanitarian corridor for stranded ships. Iran FM Araghchi vowed to permanently redesign Hormuz navigation rules. Iran executed Israeli spy Kouroush Keyvani. Iran's ballistic missile launch rate down 90% from Day 1 peak. No ceasefire imminent — Trump rebuffed Middle East mediation efforts.
footerNote: Data compiled from 100+ sources across 4 media poles as of March 19, 2026. All contested claims are marked. Multi-pole sourcing active.
